title: SmoreSpawn
description: What if OreSpawn was a little more ✨Whimsical?✨
body:
_I would recommend installing WAILA or a similar mod before using this one. I explain what some of the stuff is on this page, but I don't provide all the pictures and stats, so you might want to see that in-game._

This mod adds a lot of stuff. If you like stuff, then this mod is for you!

## But... what KIND of stuff?

Okay, let me break it down for you.

![Me when I'm breaking it down for you:](https://media1.tenor.com/m/iSHCWVSYZnoAAAAd/sonic-sonic-the-hedgehog.gif)

We have:
- Mobs! Like, a lot of them. Pretty much the whole point of this mod
- New equipment! (not a lot of it. and yes, it's quite hard to get!)
- Items! They exist mostly to support the equipment, though
- And one super special block!

## Mobs

### Apple Cow

These are red-colored cows which drop apples. Wait a couple of minutes for one to drop an apple, or kill it and get a few apples immediately. If you breed apple cows, there's a small chance that the baby will be a golden apple cow.

### Golden Apple Cow

This is a golden cow that drops golden apples (or, rarely, an enchanted golden apple). Like apple cows, you don't have to kill these: wait 5-10 minutes for a golden apple to drop, leaving the cow unharmed. You might not want to wait that long, though...

### Bird

An ambient mob that fills the skies. They're like bats, but they show up anywhere that other animals do, and they drop a single feather upon death.

### Butterfly

These are like birds, but only spawn where flowers can be found. They don't drop anything. They are pretty though, right?

### Ostrich

Ostriches are desert-dwelling animals that don't show up in large groups. They drop huge amounts of meat and feathers, but they put up a fight before you can get any of that out of them.

### Cool Kids

These aren't like the other ostriches. They wear the coolest shoes, hang out in groups, and are considerably more powerful than regular ostriches. Kill one for great rewards!

### Builder

It looks like a regular person who got lost in your world. They're a bit clueless, so they just wander around and build stuff randomly. Builders are not afraid to defend themselves and their allies!

### Hellhound

A wolf variant that spawns in the nether. They can cause fire damage and hunt down magma cubes in the wild. If you take one into the overworld, be careful around water!

## Items

Nether stars now have more than one use! Exciting, right?

### Smore

A basic, not-too-useful food source. Craft these with cookies and sugar.

### Raw/Cooked Ostrich

Extremely big piece of meat from an ostrich. This restores more hunger than all other meats.

### Star Fragment

Small pieces of a nether star. You can obtain four of these by breaking down a single nether star, but be careful, because you can't make another nether star without a catalyst. You can also get a random amount by breaking a meteorite.

Using star fragments is the only way to repair ancient tools and armor.

### Shattered Star

A "fake" nether star made from four star fragments. To make this into a real nether star, you need a catalyst.

### Star Catalyst

Made from a diamond and blaze powder, this powerful object can form a shattered star into a real nether star. These are consumed in crafting nether stars.

### Ancient tools and armor

A full set of "ancient" tools and armor can be assembled from pieces scattered randomly around the world. Most of the ancient gear you find is severely damaged, so you need to get star fragments before you can use it. It's more powerful and durable than diamond, but with less potential for enchanting. Pieces in the armor set are meant to be changed out for weaker ones with special effects and powers. As long as you have at least one ancient piece in your set, you get more protection than if that piece was diamond.

## Blocks

To be honest, there's not a whole lot of these...

### Meteorite

These show up in the nether and emit a distinct glow (is it really that distinct? There's a lot of glowy stuff in the nether). Mine them with a diamond pickaxe or better to get a few star fragments.

## Feature Wishlist

There's a lot of stuff I want to add, but haven't quite figured out yet, like:
- A hang glider held item that makes it easier to traverse wide, open spaces
- Natural spawning for builder formations and hellhound packs
- Mob spawning properly weighted by biome (all biomes use the same weight currently, skewing the more sparse ones like Plains and Extreme Hills towards custom mobs over vanilla mobs)
- Integration with economy mods such as Voxels for items with no crafting recipe
- A working rubber duck mob that floats on water (partially implemented since v1.0.0)
- General AI improvements to support water mobs and more complex behaviors
- Name tag-/scoreboard-style health display for mobs (will likely be split off into its own mod)
